title: rhamnus tomentella subsp. ursina
intro: Rhamnus tomentella subsp. ursina, also known as the 'bearberry' or 'kinnikinnick', is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. The plant's leaves and berries contain medicinal properties, including anthraquinones and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract issues, such as kidney stones and infections. The health benefits of Rhamnus tomentella subsp. ursina include its potential to reduce inflammation, act as a diuretic, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any medical condition.
